source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.topic: Expanded noun phrases for description and specification
source_alignment.source_statement: Learn how to use expanded noun phrases to describe and specify, for example the blue butterfly, plain flour and the man in the moon; use commas to separate items in a list where appropriate.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches children to find the noun first, then add one or two useful details that help a reader picture or identify it. It separates noun-phrase building from sentence punctuation and does not reward adjective quantity, handwriting speed or one preferred description.

objective.child_goal: I can add useful words to tell my reader more about a noun.
objective.statement: Build and use clear expanded noun phrases with age-appropriate determiners, adjectives and simple specifying details.
misconceptions: Calls every describing word an adjective without finding the noun it describes.
Adds a list of repeated or unrelated adjectives because a longer phrase seems better.
Places describing words after the noun in a way that sounds unnatural in the intended sentence.
Uses commas around every adjective or between the determiner and noun.
Builds a detailed noun phrase but loses the sentence capital letter, verb or end punctuation.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word that names a person, place, animal, thing or idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: A noun is a naming word. It tells us who or what.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A central naming card with person, place, animal and object icons.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A noun with the words that belong with it.
vocabulary.audio_script: A noun phrase is built around a noun.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A noun tile inside a phrase frame.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A noun phrase with useful detail added.
vocabulary.audio_script: An expanded noun phrase tells the reader more about the noun.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A noun tile gaining one or two labelled detail tiles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word that can describe a noun.
vocabulary.audio_script: An adjective can tell us what a noun is like.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A detail card pointing to the noun it describes.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word such as a, an, the, this or my that begins a noun phrase.
vocabulary.audio_script: A determiner helps point to which noun we mean.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A small pointing card at the start of a phrase.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which word names the thing: the small boat?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure the noun as the anchor before adding or removing details.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ask who or what the phrase is about. Boat is the noun.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The noun tile sits in a fixed centre slot while the and small sit in labelled outer slots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Change 'the bag' so your reader can find the right bag.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce expansion as useful meaning, not simply adding more words.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Choose details that help the reader picture or identify the noun.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Several bag cards differ by colour, size and pattern; selected details narrow the match.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model determiner, purposeful adjectives, noun order and an age-appropriate comma between two listed adjectives.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Start with the. Add small and spotted. End with the noun dog. Read the whole phrase to check it sounds right.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four labelled tiles occupy DETERMINER, DETAIL, DETAIL and NOUN slots with a comma card between the details.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose one or two details for the noun, build the phrase, then hear it inside a sentence.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise natural order, useful selection and oral checking without handwriting demands.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build the phrase. Listen to the sentence. Remove any detail that repeats or does not help.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A phrase builder feeds into a short sentence strip with replay and undo.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Is 'the shiny red bike' a noun phrase or a complete sentence?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check that expansion does not blur the difference between a noun phrase and a complete sentence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The phrase names the bike, but it does not yet tell us what the bike does or is.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The phrase fills a noun slot while an empty verb slot remains visible.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why 'the lovely nice amazing hat' is not always the clearest choice.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Repair adjective piling and secure reader-focused editing.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Tell Pip which detail helps the reader and which words repeat the same idea.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Detail cards sort into HELPS THE READER, REPEATS and DOES NOT FIT.

manipulatives.purpose: Build noun phrases around a fixed noun anchor and test each phrase for meaning, order and sound.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners select a noun, then add determiner and detail tiles by tap, drag, keyboard or switch scan. Each tile has a spoken role. Read-back places the phrase in a short sentence; undo preserves the base noun.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Oral composition, symbol-supported roles, large targets, adjustable spacing, line focus, reduced choices and adult scribing are available. Colour is supplementary, and handwriting is never the only response.

manipulatives.purpose: Compare details by whether they help a reader identify or picture the noun.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children state a reader purpose, inspect two to four candidate details, and sort each as helpful, repeated or unrelated before hearing the revised phrase.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Picture and audio cues can be toggled independently; a linear yes/no route replaces sorting; no task judges descriptions of people's bodies, worth or identity.

practice.interleaving_notes: Begin with finding nouns and adding one concrete detail. Add two natural details and comma decisions only after phrase order is secure; then interleave phrase/sentence distinction and short sentence editing in narrative and simple non-fiction.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify the noun anchor and distinguish a noun phrase from a complete sentence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short determiner-detail-noun phrase + noun highlight + optional verb-slot check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use familiar decodable vocabulary and accept spoken, pointed or selected responses.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Add one purposeful adjective or simple specifying detail to a noun phrase.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: reader purpose + base noun + 3 detail choices + phrase read-back in a sentence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Allow more than one answer when details are genuinely useful and record the learner's stated purpose.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build natural Year 2 noun phrases with two useful adjectives and an appropriate comma where they form a list.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: determiner + two reviewed adjective tiles + noun + oral naturalness and punctuation check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review must reject awkward forced adjective orders and avoid presenting comma use as a universal rule for every modifier sequence.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Remove repeated, vague or unrelated modifiers while preserving the writer's intended meaning.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: overloaded phrase + stated reader need + keep/remove choices + revised read-back
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not reward phrase length or one house style; descriptions of people must be respectful and non-stereotyping.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use expanded noun phrases inside complete punctuated sentences during spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + phrase tile + missing verb/capital/end mark decision + oral rehearsal
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep sentence load short, provide audio, and separate composition evidence from spelling or handwriting.

body.prompt: Which word is the noun in 'the tiny green frog'?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Which word names the animal?
Tiny and green tell us more about it.
question_variants.explanation: Frog is the noun because it names the animal; tiny and green describe the frog.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "frog". Frog is the noun because it names the animal; tiny and green describe the frog.
feedback.repair: Which word names the animal? Tiny and green tell us more about it. Use that clue to repair detail called noun, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this phrase or sentence, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "frog": Frog is the noun because it names the animal; tiny and green describe the frog.
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Build a clear phrase for a dog that is small and spotted.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Start with the and end with dog.
Put the two describing words before the noun.
question_variants.explanation: The phrase starts with a determiner, adds two useful details and ends with the noun dog.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "the small, spotted dog"; acceptable spoken without punctuation: "the small spotted dog". The phrase starts with a determiner, adds two useful details and ends with the noun dog.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Calls every describing word an adjective without finding the noun it describes.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner selects a detail word as the noun or cannot say who or what the phrase names.
repair_sequence: Ask who or what the phrase is about.
Place that naming word in the fixed noun slot.
Attach each remaining word only if it belongs with and tells more about the noun.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner finds the noun in a new phrase and points from each adjective to that noun.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds a list of repeated or unrelated adjectives because a longer phrase seems better.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner selects several praise words or details that do not support the reader's stated need.
repair_sequence: State what the reader needs to know.
Sort details into helpful, repeated and unrelated.
Keep one or two details and read the shorter phrase aloud.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner builds a fresh phrase and explains how each retained detail helps.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Places describing words after the noun in a way that sounds unnatural in the intended sentence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner builds sequences such as 'the dog small spotted' and accepts the read-back.
repair_sequence: Lock the determiner and noun into start and end slots.
Place one detail immediately before the noun.
Read both versions aloud and choose the natural order.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner orders a new determiner-detail-noun phrase and checks it orally.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses commas around every adjective or between the determiner and noun.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner inserts punctuation after each tile or writes forms such as 'the, small, dog'.
repair_sequence: Remove all punctuation and rebuild the phrase words.
Listen for two details working as a list.
Add only the reviewed comma and compare the clean phrase.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner punctuates a new reviewed two-adjective phrase without separating determiner from noun.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Builds a detailed noun phrase but loses the sentence capital letter, verb or end punctuation.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner submits an expanded phrase when the task asks for a complete sentence.
repair_sequence: Place the complete noun phrase in the sentence's who-or-what slot.
Add a verb phrase that tells what happens.
Check the capital letter and end mark after reading the whole sentence.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner uses a new expanded noun phrase inside a complete spoken or constructed sentence.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm writing desk, stable tiles, muted focus outlines and no shaking, red failure marks or animated word clouds.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace tile movement and detail filtering with numbered static phrase stages and before-and-after sentences.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read every word, phrase and completed sentence neutrally; allow oral rehearsal, slow replay and spoken answers before print choices.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Offer adjustable spacing, line focus, symbol-backed word roles, short decodable examples and a persistent noun/detail glossary.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use a fixed FIND NOUN-ADD ONE DETAIL-READ-CHECK routine and reveal only the current decision.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited time, undo, reduced choices, sentence frames and adult scribing without lowering the language objective.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Accept varied respectful descriptions, credit concise phrases and describe revision as helping the reader rather than fixing bad writing.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach noun, describe, detail, phrase and determiner with objects and pictures; allow oral rehearsal and home-language discussion because adjective order may differ across languages.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires identifying the noun and building purposeful expanded noun phrases in supported sentences; secure requires retained selection, order, punctuation and sentence transfer across oral, tile and editing formats.
evidence.parent_summary: Can add useful describing words to a noun and use the expanded phrase in a clear sentence.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Finds the noun anchor, chooses reader-helpful detail, orders the phrase naturally, explains a comma decision in reviewed examples and preserves complete sentence structure.
next_actions: Use expanded noun phrases in short descriptions and instructions.
Revisit noun identification when detail choices become unstable.
Keep spelling and handwriting support separate so motor or transcription needs do not hide composition knowledge.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 expanded noun phrase pack with a deterministic 220-item pilot bank covering noun identification, purposeful construction, reviewed two-detail punctuation, reader-focused editing, oral rehearsal and transfer into complete sentences. Optional narration references require ElevenLabs production and human listening approval; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent English, SEND, bias, audio and renderer review remain required before promotion.
